metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan title to '17 residual fsck faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)' so it matches the Context, Forensics, and ACs.
findings.finding: The plan title states '14 residual fsck orphans (13 EDIT ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE)' but the Context section reports '17 such faults: 13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE', and both the Forensic classification and AC2 confirm 4 MISSING_CREATE tickets (110e, 957d, 9fa4, cf5d). The title's counts (14 total, 1 MISSING_CREATE) directly contradict every other section.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Context section vs. AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'Human-triage the 17 residual fsck faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the title to match the body's actual fault count: '17 residual fsck faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)' (or equivalent accurate summary).
findings.finding: The title states '14 residual fsck orphans (13 EDIT ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE)' but the plan body reports 17 faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE) and the acceptance criteria address all 4 MISSING_CREATE tickets (110e, 957d, 9fa4, cf5d). The headline undercounts the MISSING_CREATE count by 3, creating a mismatch between what the title promises and what the body actually does.
findings.location: Title vs. Context section and AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Update the ticket title to: 'Human-triage the 17 residual fsck faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan title to match the body: update '14 residual fsck orphans (13 EDIT ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE)' to '17 residual fsck orphans (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)' so the title-level edit-set count matches AC2's four phantom-dir deletion targets.
findings.finding: The plan title states '14 residual fsck orphans (13 EDIT ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE)' but the body (Context paragraph) states '17 such faults: 13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE', and AC2 enumerates four phantom dirs to delete (110e, 957d, 9fa4, cf5d). The title misrepresents the MISSING_CREATE count as 1 (not 4) and the total as 14 (not 17), causing a scope mismatch between the title-level edit-set description and the actual work defined in the ACs.
findings.location: Plan title and Context paragraph vs. AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Rename ticket title to: 'Human-triage the 17 residual fsck orphans (13 EDIT ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the rename loop is idempotent (e.g., skip-if-already-renamed: proceed only when `.json` source exists, treat absence as already-done) so a partially-completed run is safely re-runnable.
findings.finding: The `.json` → `.json.retired` rename step is not stated to be idempotent/resumable. If the 13-rename sequence is partially applied (e.g., 7 of 13 renamed) and then re-run, the already-renamed files no longer exist at their original paths, so a naive re-run will either error on missing source files or silently skip them — neither outcome is acknowledged.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to AC line 1: 'The rename is idempotent — if `<uuid>.json` does not exist but `<uuid>.json.retired` already does, treat the step as complete and continue. After all 13 renames, verify with `rebar fsck` that ORPHAN_EVENT count is 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-mutation step to quiesce the store (e.g., pause the reconciler process, take a write lock, or operate on a copy) before the 13 sequential renames, to prevent the known write race from interfering with or invalidating the repair.
findings.finding: The plan performs 13 sequential file renames (orphan retirement) against a live store whose root cause is an active compaction-vs-reconciler write race, but specifies no store quiesce/lock or reconciler pause before or during the mutation sequence. A concurrent reconciler or compaction write during the rename sequence could create new ORPHAN_EVENT faults or observe partially-retired state.
findings.location: AC — 'Per-orphan disposition applied: retire each of the 13 orphan EDIT files'; Notes — 'Root cause (compaction snapshotting before observing a remote reconciler write) is a recurring race'
findings.suggested_fix: Before renaming any .json files, pause the reconciler (or set a maintenance flag the reconciler respects), perform all 13 renames atomically as a batch, run `rebar fsck` to confirm zero faults, then resume the reconciler. Document the quiesce/resume steps in the ticket comment alongside the forensic classification.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the retirement script must be idempotent (skip files already named .json.retired, attempt all 13 regardless of individual errors) and document the re-entry procedure if interrupted, so a partial run can be safely resumed without restoring from backup.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify a partial-failure recovery procedure if the 13-rename sequence is interrupted mid-way (e.g., filesystem error after 7 of 13 renames). Re-running a naive rename script would fail on already-renamed files (source no longer exists), and the plan does not state whether the retirement script is idempotent or how to resume from a known checkpoint.
findings.location: AC — 'Per-orphan disposition applied: retire each of the 13 orphan EDIT files (.json -> .json.retired)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use a script that iterates all 13 target files, skips any already ending in .retired, and logs each rename result (success/skip/error) before proceeding to the next. A final `rebar fsck` confirms completeness. This makes re-runs safe and gives the operator a clear audit trail of which renames succeeded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before deleting the 4 phantom dirs, confirm each is git-untracked at HEAD (e.g. `git ls-files --error-unmatch <dir>` returns non-zero, or `git show HEAD:<dir>` shows no entries) and explicitly stage the deletion (`git rm -r --cached <dir>` if needed) so the committed tree matches the filesystem state.
findings.finding: The MISSING_CREATE phantom-dir deletion is predicated on the assertion that all 4 dirs (110e, 957d, 9fa4, cf5d) have '0 tracked files, not in HEAD', but the plan specifies no pre-deletion verification step to confirm this. If any dir has even one git-tracked file (or is present in the remote HEAD), a plain filesystem delete without a matching `git rm` leaves git's index out of sync; a subsequent `git pull`/merge would re-materialize the dir and resurrect the MISSING_CREATE fault, making the remediation non-durable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-deletion step: `git ls-files tickets/<dir>/` should return empty for each of the 4 dirs; if any returns files, use `git rm -r tickets/<dir>/` rather than a plain filesystem delete to ensure the removal is reflected in the committed tree and won't be resurrected on pull.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC3 for the before/after status diff (e.g., a `jq` query against the existing NDJSON export compared to a post-remediation re-export, or a `rebar show` loop over the 13 UUIDs) so the 'no status/assignee changed' claim is falsifiable.
findings.finding: AC3 claims 'verified via a before/after diff of all 13 orphan tickets' but specifies no concrete command or mechanism to produce the before-state output for comparison, leaving the diff step unexecutable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'verified via a before/after diff of all 13 orphan tickets — all `closed`; assignees `""` except cc5b and f112 = "Joe Oakhart"'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Proved by: `for uuid in <13 uuids>; do rebar show $uuid | jq -r \'[.uuid,.status,.assignee]\'; done` run before and after — output must be identical; diff produced by `diff <(before-output) <(after-output)` must be empty.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback step to the plan (e.g., 'To undo: git checkout 8c3c26f72 -- <path> to restore any deleted dir from the bundle') so the irreversible deletions have a documented back-out procedure.
findings.finding: The deletion of the 4 phantom directories (110e, 957d, 9fa4, cf5d) is a destructive, irreversible file-system operation. The plan names a backup (188MB git bundle + NDJSON export at HEAD 8c3c26f72) but never articulates an explicit rollback/restore procedure — how to extract and re-materialize the deleted dirs from that backup if the deletion turns out to be wrong.
findings.location: Context (backup mention) vs. AC line 2 (delete 4 phantom dirs) + Notes
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'To restore any deleted phantom dir: `git checkout 8c3c26f72 -- tickets/<uuid>/` against the bundle at 8c3c26f72, then re-run fsck to confirm no regressions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ticket `f112` exists in the store (or clarify its full UUID) and confirm its assignee is 'Joe Oakhart' before relying on it as an AC3 diff baseline; replace the short prefix with the full ticket UUID to make the benchmark unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts the before/after diff will cover 'all 13 orphan tickets — all `closed`; assignees `""` except cc5b and f112 = "Joe Oakhart"'. The ticket prefix `f112` yields zero results in the entire repo (no ticket file, no event file, no test fixture reference), making the stated assignee for f112 an unverifiable claim that forms part of the AC3 correctness check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 — '...assignees "" except cc5b and f112 = "Joe Oakhart"'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `f112` in AC3 with the full ticket UUID (e.g., `f112-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx`) and add a `rebar show f112-…` output snippet to the forensic comment so the before-state is pinned.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure that names the recovery artefact (e.g., 'restore tickets branch to commit 8c3c26f72 from the git bundle') and asserts the expected post-rollback store state (e.g., 'fsck reports the original 17 faults, no phantom dirs deleted').
findings.finding: The plan asserts a backup exists but never states the schema/store STATE after a rollback from a partial execution. The T11 spec requires the plan to assert the post-rollback schema state, not merely that a rollback artefact exists. For the four destructive `rm -rf` directory deletions in particular, if two of the four dirs are deleted and the operation is aborted, the plan gives no explicit procedure to restore the store to a known-good state (e.g., 'restore from git bundle to branch HEAD 8c3c26f72').
findings.location: AC line 2 (MISSING_CREATE disposition) and Notes/Context (backup mention)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If the operation is aborted mid-run, restore the tickets branch: `git fetch <bundle>; git reset --hard 8c3c26f72`. Post-rollback assertion: `rebar fsck` must report exactly 17 faults (13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE), matching the pre-run baseline.'
